summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of KaamHub V1.0. Backend pytest suite (26 tests) covers auth, admin protection, seed data, full booking + partner verification + withdrawal flow, services CRUD — ALL 26 PASSED. Frontend Playwright covers splash/landing, customer register → 4-step booking → confirmation (booking persisted), admin login → dashboard KPIs, partner register → onboarding submission → status pending, admin verifications approve, admin payouts page, admin services page — ALL PASSED.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout after 5 failed logins (mentioned in playbook_testing_instructions) is NOT implemented in /api/auth/login. Currently unlimited login attempts allowed.
Unauthenticated calls correctly return 401, but the frontend AuthContext appears to call /api/auth/me on every page load even when no session — produces noisy 401 console errors. Cosmetic.

When CORS_ORIGINS="*" is set in .env, code falls back to FRONTEND_URL only. allow_credentials=True + allow_origins=["*"] would be invalid per CORS spec; current logic handles it, but the .env wildcard is misleading. Consider documenting that explicit origins must be set.
No state-machine guard — partner can jump from accepted directly to completed (skip on_the_way/started). Wallet still credits. Consider enforcing ordered transitions.

issues: Hero image is a flat orange/navy diagonal gradient placeholder, not a real home-services photo. Brand-consistent but feels stock.
Two 401s logged in console on initial load (from /api/auth/me probe) for anonymous users. Consider suppressing logging or skipping the probe when no auth cookie is present.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Implement brute-force account lockout after 5 failed login attempts (referenced in auth playbook).
OPTIONAL: Enforce booking status state-machine (pending → accepted → on_the_way → started → completed) on backend.
OPTIONAL: Suppress unauthenticated /api/auth/me 401 console errors on landing page.
OPTIONAL: Replace flat gradient hero image with a real home-services photo.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 895 lines — close to the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/bookings.py, routers/admin.py for maintainability.
Booking status update endpoint does not validate transition order — any partner can mark a booking completed directly, which credits wallet. Add state-machine validation.
No rate limiting / brute-force protection on /api/auth/login.
`admin_stats today_bookings` and `monthly_revenue` use regex over the ISO string `created_at` field — works but fragile; consider storing `created_at` as native datetime + range queries.

set_auth_cookies hardcodes secure=True samesite=none, which means local non-HTTPS testing breaks. The pytest suite needed to use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (public HTTPS) — backend has no LOCAL_DEV toggle. Document this for local devs.
FastAPI startup event uses deprecated @app.on_event; switch to lifespan handlers for FastAPI ≥0.95.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@kaamhub.in / Admin@12345. Test users created dynamically per run with TEST_/uitest_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Test created several uitest_cust_*/uitest_part_* users and one booking + approved partner against the live preview backend. Cleanup not performed (test data is suffixed and harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses httpOnly secure cookies with samesite=none. Local pytest from inside container against http://localhost:8001 will FAIL because requests-lib won't send Secure cookies over http. Use the REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (https preview URL) — the test file already auto-reads it from /app/frontend/.env. pytest config (/app/backend/pytest.ini) forces -n 2 --dist loadscope; module-scoped fixtures may run twice (once per worker), so the register fixture uses register-or-login fallback. Test data: emails prefixed uitest_/test_ with random hex suffix per run.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bugs found. Initial pytest failures were due to (a) localhost http vs Secure cookies (resolved by reading REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env) and (b) module-scoped register fixture running on both xdist workers and hitting duplicate-email (resolved with register-or-login fallback).
